questions.name: Field Completeness
questions.description: Proportion of mandatory schema fields populated including core identification, hierarchical structure, governance, and composition metadata
questions.evidence: Core fields present: id (DOI), title, description, keywords (25), license_and_use_terms, page, creators (15), purposes (3), instances (4), funders, subpopulations (7), collection_mechanisms (4), acquisition_methods (4), preprocessing_strategies (5), cleaning_strategies (3), intended_uses (6), discouraged_uses (3), distribution_formats (5), maintainers, updates, external_resources (12). Missing: version, publisher, citation, conforms_to, format, encoding, variables, is_tabular, resources, parent_datasets, related_datasets, confidentiality_level, regulatory_restrictions
questions.quality_note: Strong field completion with comprehensive content in all major D4D sections. Primary gaps in structured metadata fields (version, publisher, citation) that exist in text but not as structured elements, and hierarchical/governance fields
questions.semantic_analysis: Approximately 85% field completion - excellent coverage of motivation, composition, collection, preprocessing, uses, distribution, and maintenance sections

questions.name: Entry Length Adequacy
questions.description: Checks whether narrative fields (e.g., description, purposes) have meaningful content length
questions.evidence: description: 1,383 characters (23 lines); purposes[0].description: 430 chars; purposes[1].description: 386 chars; purposes[2].description: 339 chars; tasks descriptions: 200-400 chars each; collection_mechanisms descriptions: 300-600 chars each
questions.quality_note: Excellent narrative field length throughout - all major descriptions exceed 200 characters with rich detail
questions.semantic_analysis: All narrative fields substantially exceed minimum length requirement

questions.name: Keyword Diversity
questions.description: Number of unique keywords provided to describe dataset topic coverage
questions.evidence: keywords: 25 unique keywords spanning methodologies (Cell Maps, AP-MS, SEC-MS, Immunofluorescence, Mass Spectrometry, Perturb-Seq), cell lines (MDA-MB-468, KOLF2.1J, iPSC), disease contexts (Breast Cancer), technologies (CRISPR Perturbation, Deep Learning, Visible Neural Networks), and standards (FAIR Principles, RO-Crate, FAIRSCAPE)
questions.quality_note: Exceptional keyword diversity covering all major dataset dimensions
questions.semantic_analysis: Well above threshold with comprehensive coverage of methods, biological systems, diseases, and standards

questions.name: File Enumeration and Type Variety
questions.description: Number of distribution formats and file type diversity
questions.evidence: distribution_formats: 5 formats - (1) RO-Crate packages with provenance, (2) Mass spec data in MassIVE, (3) Sequence data in NCBI SRA, (4) Hierarchical cell maps in NDEx, (5) Archives in UVA Dataverse. Multiple data types: imaging (IF confocal microscopy), proteomics (AP-MS, SEC-MS), genomics (CRISPR screens, single-cell RNA-seq), network data (hierarchical DAGs)
questions.quality_note: Highly multimodal dataset with 5 distinct distribution platforms and 4+ data modalities
questions.semantic_analysis: Excellent file type variety representing multimodal integration of imaging, proteomics, and genomics data

questions.name: Data File Size Availability
questions.description: Presence of file size or dimensional metadata (bytes, instance counts, data splits)
questions.evidence: instances: 4 documented (2 cell lines, 100 chromatin regulators, 100 metabolic enzymes); subpopulations: 7 conditions; subsets describe: 563 proteins imaged (IF), 17 genes tagged for AP-MS, 72/100 chromatin modifiers in SEC-MS, 11,739 genes in CRISPR atlas, >1,000 complexes detected; no bytes field for file sizes
questions.quality_note: Instance counts and sample dimensions well-documented; file sizes not provided
questions.semantic_analysis: Pass - dimensional metadata present through instance and subset descriptions

questions.name: Dataset Identification Metadata
questions.description: Presence of unique identifiers such as DOI, RRID, or persistent URLs, AND hosting platform identification
questions.score_label: Pass - Multiple DOIs and RRIDs present
questions.evidence: id: https://doi.org/10.18130/V3/DXWOS5 (primary DOI); Additional DOIs: 10.18130/V3/B35XWX (V1.4), 10.18130/V3/F3TD5R (V2.1), 10.1101/2024.05.21.589311 (bioRxiv); RRIDs: RRID:CVCL_0419 (MDA-MB-468), RRID:CVCL_B5P3 (KOLF2.1J); Hosting platforms mentioned: UVA Dataverse, MassIVE, NCBI SRA/BioProject, NDEx; no publisher field
questions.quality_note: Excellent identifier coverage with multiple DOIs and valid RRIDs; hosting platforms described but not in publisher field
questions.semantic_analysis: Pass - comprehensive persistent identifier coverage; publisher field missing but platforms documented

questions.name: Funding and Acknowledgements Completeness
questions.description: Checks presence of funding sources, grants, institutional sponsors, and creator affiliations
questions.score_label: Complete funding with grants + creators with affiliations
questions.evidence: funders: NIH Common Fund Bridge2AI Program with grants 1OT2OD032742-01 and 5U54HG012513-02, Opportunity Number OTA-21-008, FY 2025 funding amounts ($5,289,382), project dates (Sep 2022 - Aug 2026), Frederick Thomas Fund; creators: 15 creators with roles (Contact PI, Co-Investigator) and affiliations (UCSD, SFU, UVA, UAB, UCSF, Stanford, UMontreal, Yale, UT Austin)
questions.quality_note: Comprehensive funding documentation with grant numbers, amounts, dates, and complete creator affiliations
questions.semantic_analysis: Exemplary funding transparency with complete grant details and creator attribution

questions.name: Ethical and Privacy Declarations
questions.description: Comprehensive ethics coverage including IRB, deidentification, privacy, consent, compensation, vulnerable populations, DPIA, and re-identification risk
questions.score_label: Basic ethics (IRB + deidentification) + vulnerable population info
questions.evidence: human_subject_research: involves_human_subjects=false, Ethics Module (Vardit Ravitsky, Jean-Christophe Bélisle-Pipon), Data Access Committee (Jillian Parker), Bridge2AI Ethics Working Group, de-identification status described ('cannot be matched to human subject'), special_populations (MDA-MB-468: 51-yo black female, KOLF2.1J: healthy male Northern European); sensitive_elements: de-identified cell lines; Missing: is_deidentified field, data_protection_impacts, participant_privacy, reidentification_risk, informed_consent, participant_compensation
questions.quality_note: Ethics oversight well-documented with named reviewers; de-identification described; missing structured fields for DPIA, privacy protections, and re-identification risk (though less critical for cell line data)
questions.semantic_analysis: Good ethics documentation appropriate for cell line data; could enhance with structured privacy/DPIA fields

questions.name: Access Requirements and Governance Documentation
questions.description: Determines if access policy, license, IP restrictions, regulatory restrictions, confidentiality level, multi-jurisdiction compliance, and governance contacts are clearly defined
questions.evidence: license_and_use_terms: Comprehensive CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 with attribution requirements, non-commercial use only, commercial licensing available through UCSD/Stanford/UCSF, Data Access Committee oversight, must cite bioRxiv publication and data DOI; Missing: regulatory_restrictions, confidentiality_level, hipaa_compliant, other_compliance, governance_committee_contact (though Data Access Committee mentioned in text)
questions.quality_note: Strong license documentation with governance oversight mentioned; lacks structured fields for regulatory compliance, confidentiality classification, and governance contact
questions.semantic_analysis: License well-specified; governance present but not in structured fields; missing multi-jurisdiction compliance metadata

questions.name: Interoperability, Standardization, and Cross-Platform Integration
questions.description: Presence of standard formats, ontologies, schema conformance (e.g., Parquet, TSV, LinkML), cross-platform linkages with typed relationships, AND dataset integration capability
questions.evidence: Standards mentioned: FAIR principles, RO-Crate (Research Object Crate), Schema.org, EVI Evidence Graph Ontology, Gene Ontology, Reactome, PDB, AlphaFold, JSON-Schema, JSON-LD; Integration: MuSIC pipeline integrates multimodal data (IF imaging, AP-MS, SEC-MS, CRISPR screens); FAIRSCAPE framework for AI-readiness; Missing: format, encoding, conforms_to, conforms_to_schema fields (standards described in text but not structured)
questions.quality_note: Excellent standards adoption and integration capability via MuSIC pipeline and FAIRSCAPE; lacks structured conformance declarations
questions.semantic_analysis: Strong interoperability with multiple standards and integration framework; would benefit from structured conforms_to field

questions.name: Tool and Software Transparency
questions.description: Mentions of preprocessing, cleaning, and labeling strategies with software tools used in data preparation, including annotation quality metrics and imputation protocols
questions.evidence: preprocessing_strategies: 5 strategies with tools (node2vec for PPI, Human Protein Atlas deep learning model for imaging, contrastive deep learning, Cytoscape for community detection, GO/Reactome/LLM for annotation); cleaning_strategies: 3 strategies (FAIRSCAPE-CLI for packaging, data standard mapping, integrative modeling pipeline); Software mentioned throughout: MuSIC pipeline, FAIRSCAPE, Cytoscape, HPA model, node2vec; Missing: software_and_tools field, annotation_analyses, machine_annotation_tools, imputation_protocols fields
questions.quality_note: Software tools comprehensively described throughout documentation with processing details; lacks structured software_and_tools field and annotation quality metrics
questions.semantic_analysis: Strong tool documentation in text form; would benefit from structured software_and_tools field with versions

questions.name: Collection Protocol Clarity
questions.description: Evaluates description completeness of data collection mechanisms, acquisition methods, data collectors, collection timeframes, and raw data sources
questions.score_label: Full protocol with methods and collectors (missing timeframes)
questions.evidence: collection_mechanisms: 4 detailed mechanisms (IF spatial proteomics with automated pipetting robot, AP-MS with endogenous tagging, SEC-MS for proteome-wide mapping, CRISPR screens with lentiviral library); acquisition_methods: 4 detailed methods (confocal microscopy 4-channel, mass spectrometry, single-cell RNA-seq with 10x Genomics 3'HT, MuSIC pipeline); Collectors implied: Lundberg Lab (Stanford), Krogan Lab (UCSF), Mali Lab (UCSD); Missing: data_collectors field, collection_timeframes field, raw_data_sources field (though source cell lines documented)
questions.quality_note: Comprehensive collection protocols with technical details and lab attributions; lacks structured fields for collectors, timeframes, and raw data sources
questions.semantic_analysis: Excellent protocol documentation; would benefit from structured collector and timeframe fields

questions.name: Version History, Maintenance, and Sustainability
questions.description: Presence of version information, version access methods, errata, update plans, release notes with dates, AND data sustainability indicators
questions.score_label: Version access + comprehensive sustainability (missing version field)
questions.evidence: updates: Quarterly release plan through Nov 2026, version history (v0.5 alpha, V1.4 beta March 2025 doi:10.18130/V3/B35XWX, V2.1 beta June 2025 doi:10.18130/V3/F3TD5R), change descriptions (perturb-seq, SEC-MS, IF images, RGB corrections), long-term preservation plan; retention_limit: LibraData (UVA Dataverse) with institutional commitment, NIH data sharing compliance; maintainers: CM4AI Consortium with 8 institutions; Sustainability: persistent identifiers (DOI/ARK), domain repository (Dataverse), institutional funding; Missing: version field, version_access field (though DOIs provide access)
questions.quality_note: Strong version tracking and comprehensive sustainability plan; lacks structured version and version_access fields
questions.semantic_analysis: Excellent sustainability with long-term preservation and update schedule; would benefit from version field

questions.name: Associated Publications
questions.description: Presence of formal citations or DOI-linked references
questions.evidence: external_resources: bioRxiv publication (Clark T, et al. Cell Maps for AI, doi:10.1101/2024.05.21.589311), Perturbation Cell Atlas publication (Nourreddine S, et al, doi:10.1101/2024.11.03.621734, PMCID: PMC11580897); license_and_use_terms requires citation of bioRxiv article and data DOI; Missing: citation field with formatted citation string, citation instructions not structured
questions.quality_note: Publications linked with DOIs and citation required in license terms; lacks structured citation field with formatted string
questions.semantic_analysis: Good publication linkage; missing structured citation field (mandatory for Bridge2AI)

questions.name: Human Subject Representation
questions.description: Indicates inclusion of human subjects with demographic diversity, subpopulation details, vulnerable population protections, and missing data documentation
questions.score_label: General human data with subgroup description
questions.evidence: instances: 2 human-derived cell lines (MDA-MB-468 from 51-yo black female, KOLF2.1J from healthy male Northern European); subpopulations: 7 conditions (3 MDA-MB-468 treatments, 4 KOLF2.1J differentiation states); human_subject_research.special_populations documents demographic origins; Missing: vulnerable_populations field, is_subpopulation field, missing_data_documentation field
questions.quality_note: Cell line origins documented with demographic context; not primary human subjects but diversity represented; lacks structured missing data documentation
questions.semantic_analysis: Appropriate for cell line data with demographic context; not direct human subjects

questions.name: Findability (Persistent Links)
questions.description: Dataset includes persistent URLs, DOI, and identifier for access and documentation
questions.evidence: id: https://doi.org/10.18130/V3/DXWOS5; page: https://www.cm4ai.org; Additional DOIs for versions: 10.18130/V3/B35XWX, 10.18130/V3/F3TD5R; ARK identifiers mentioned in FAIRSCAPE framework; RRIDs for cell lines
questions.quality_note: Excellent findability with multiple persistent identifiers across different systems
questions.semantic_analysis: Pass - comprehensive persistent identifier coverage

questions.name: Accessibility (Access Mechanism)
questions.description: Describes how users can obtain the dataset (download URL, distribution formats, access policy)
questions.score_label: Fully defined access path (access tier implied but not explicit)
questions.evidence: distribution_formats: 5 platforms with access_urls (Dataverse DOIs, MassIVE, NCBI SRA/BioProject, NDEx, cm4ai.org); license_and_use_terms: CC BY-NC-SA with Data Access Committee oversight; Access tier implied: registration/DUA likely required given Data Access Committee mention; Missing: download_url field, explicit access tier classification
questions.quality_note: Access mechanisms well-documented via multiple platforms; access tier implied through governance but not explicitly stated
questions.semantic_analysis: Good access documentation; would benefit from explicit access tier and download_url field

questions.name: Reusability, Use Guidance, and Social Impact
questions.description: License clearly defined with explicit use guidance including intended uses, prohibited uses, discouraged uses, AND comprehensive social impact analysis with risk identification and mitigation strategies
questions.score_label: License + comprehensive use guidance (missing formal social impact)
questions.evidence: license_and_use_terms: CC BY-NC-SA with clear terms; intended_uses: 6 uses (AI/ML training, VNN development, genotype-phenotype mapping, drug response prediction, disease mechanisms, AI-readiness model); discouraged_uses: 3 uses (clinical decision-making without validation, use during incomplete release, analysis without domain expertise); addressing_gaps describes transformative potential for AI in genomics; Missing: future_use_impacts field with formal social impact analysis and mitigation strategies
questions.quality_note: Excellent use guidance with clear intended and discouraged uses; social impact implied through purposes and gaps but not formal RAI-aligned analysis
questions.semantic_analysis: Strong reusability guidance; would benefit from structured future_use_impacts field

questions.name: Data Integrity, Provenance Graph, and Quality
questions.description: Presence of version access, errata, update plans, source derivation, parent dataset linkages, missing data documentation, data split indicators, AND provenance graph representation
questions.score_label: Full provenance graph with FAIRSCAPE framework
questions.evidence: Provenance: FAIRSCAPE framework creates RO-Crate packages with datasets, metadata, provenance graphs (W3C PROV-O compatible), software; EVI Evidence Graph Ontology for provenance entailments; end-to-end provenance tracking from source cell lines (ATCC, HipSci) through MuSIC pipeline to hierarchical cell maps; updates: comprehensive update plan with version history; Quality: QC procedures documented for imaging and mass spec; Missing: was_derived_from field, parent_datasets field, missing_data_documentation field, is_data_split field (though provenance described)
questions.quality_note: Excellent provenance via FAIRSCAPE with full entity-activity-agent tracking; lacks structured was_derived_from and parent_datasets fields but provenance comprehensively described
questions.semantic_analysis: Strong provenance graph representation through FAIRSCAPE RO-Crate system with W3C PROV-O alignment

questions.name: Bias Documentation and Responsible AI Alignment
questions.description: Metadata documents known biases using standardized taxonomies (BiasTypeEnum, AIO) aligned with CROISSANT RAI standards, and includes fairness analysis
questions.evidence: No known_biases field present; No future_use_impacts field with fairness analysis; Potential biases not explicitly documented: selection bias (specific cell lines only - MDA-MB-468 and KOLF2.1J), population bias (limited diversity in cell line demographics - one black female donor, one Northern European male donor), representation bias (cancer and iPSC contexts only)
questions.quality_note: Missing explicit bias documentation; dataset has inherent selection and population biases that should be documented using RAI-aligned taxonomy
questions.semantic_analysis: Major gap - no bias documentation despite clear selection and population biases in cell line choices

issues_detected.description: Multiple important metadata fields present in text but not in structured format
issues_detected.recommendation: Add structured fields: version (current: 2.1), software_and_tools (MuSIC, node2vec, HPA model, Cytoscape, FAIRSCAPE-CLI), publisher (UVA Dataverse), citation (formatted string), conforms_to (FAIR, RO-Crate, Schema.org, EVI, GO, Reactome)

issues_detected.description: Governance structures mentioned but not in structured fields
issues_detected.recommendation: Add structured governance fields with Data Access Committee contact (Jillian Parker), confidentiality_level, and regulatory compliance

issues_detected.description: No bias documentation despite clear selection and population biases
issues_detected.recommendation: Document biases: selection_bias (two specific cell lines only), population_bias (limited demographic diversity: one black female, one Northern European male), representation_bias (cancer and iPSC contexts only, not representative of all cell types or diseases)

issues_detected.description: No formal social impact analysis using RAI framework
issues_detected.recommendation: Add future_use_impacts with social impact analysis: potential benefits (interpretable AI for genomics, precision medicine), risks (over-reliance on limited cell models, generalization failures), mitigation strategies (domain expertise requirements, validation in broader contexts)

issues_detected.description: Flat structure despite multiple related data products
issues_detected.recommendation: Structure subsets as hierarchical resources: IF imaging data, AP-MS data, SEC-MS data, CRISPR atlas, hierarchical cell maps as separate Dataset resources with parent relationships

issues_detected.description: No structured variable-level metadata for complex multimodal data
issues_detected.recommendation: Add variable metadata: protein abundance (AP-MS/SEC-MS), protein localization (IF imaging), gene expression (CRISPR screens), cell map nodes/edges; specify tabular structure for quantitative data

semantic_insights: Exceptional technical documentation with detailed collection mechanisms and preprocessing strategies
Strong provenance tracking through FAIRSCAPE RO-Crate framework with W3C PROV-O compatibility
Comprehensive funding transparency with complete grant details and institutional support
Excellent multimodal integration via MuSIC pipeline combining imaging, proteomics, and genomics
Robust ethics oversight appropriate for cell line data with named ethics reviewers
Multiple persistent identifiers across different systems (DOI, ARK, RRID)
Clear quarterly update plan with long-term sustainability through institutional repository
Extensive external resource linkage (12 resources) connecting to publications, tools, and repositories

strengths: Comprehensive technical transparency with detailed collection, preprocessing, and quality control procedures
Excellent provenance documentation through FAIRSCAPE framework with full W3C PROV-O graph representation
Strong funding transparency with complete grant information and institutional commitments
Multimodal data integration via MuSIC pipeline combining imaging, proteomics, and genomics
Clear licensing and use guidance with both intended and discouraged uses specified
Robust version management with quarterly updates and long-term preservation plan
Exceptional keyword diversity (25 keywords) covering all dataset dimensions
Multiple distribution formats across specialized repositories (Dataverse, MassIVE, NCBI SRA, NDEx)
Strong ethics oversight appropriate for cell line data
weaknesses: Missing critical bias documentation despite clear selection and population biases in cell line choices
No formal social impact analysis using RAI-aligned frameworks (CROISSANT)
Several important metadata fields exist in text but not as structured elements (version, publisher, citation, conforms_to, software_and_tools)
Missing governance metadata fields (confidentiality_level, regulatory_restrictions, governance contacts)
No structured variable-level metadata despite complex multimodal data
Flat dataset structure without hierarchical relationships for subsets
Access tier not explicitly stated (implied through Data Access Committee mention)
Missing structured fields for data collectors, collection timeframes, and raw data sources
recommendations: HIGH PRIORITY: Add known_biases field documenting selection_bias, population_bias, and representation_bias
HIGH PRIORITY: Add future_use_impacts field with social impact analysis and mitigation strategies aligned with CROISSANT RAI
Add structured metadata fields: version ('2.1'), publisher ('University of Virginia LibraData (Dataverse)'), citation (formatted DataCite string)
Add software_and_tools field listing: MuSIC pipeline, node2vec, Human Protein Atlas model, Cytoscape, FAIRSCAPE-CLI with versions
Add conforms_to field: ['FAIR principles', 'RO-Crate', 'Schema.org', 'EVI Evidence Graph Ontology', 'Gene Ontology', 'Reactome']
Add governance fields: confidentiality_level, regulatory_restrictions, governance_committee_contact (Jillian Parker, Data Access Committee)
Add variables field with protein abundances, localization data, gene expression profiles, cell map structure
Consider restructuring subsets as hierarchical Dataset resources with parent_datasets relationships
Add explicit access tier classification (likely: DUA required)
Add download_url field pointing to primary data access location
Add structured fields for data_collectors, collection_timeframes, raw_data_sources
Add annotation_analyses field documenting GO/Reactome alignment and LLM annotation quality
Add is_tabular and is_data_split flags for quantitative data components
